title: Vireo's New Norwegian LBG Plant
content: Dutch technology company Nordsol has been selected by Norwegian renewable energy developer Vireo to deliver a new liquefied biogas (LBG, bio-LNG) production plant in Hardanger. The facility will be Nordsolâ€™s first project in Norway. It will use the companyâ€™s proven technology to convert biogas, produced on-site from salmon processing residues and poultry manure, into high-quality LBG for use in road transport and maritime sectors. Construction has started in Q2 2025, and commissioning is planned for Q3 2026. The project will process around 125,000 tons of organic feedstock per year, producing approximately 90 GWh of renewable LBG annually. All LBG from the plant will be supplied to the Nordic energy company Gasum under a long-term offtake agreement. At the heart of the new facility is Nordsolâ€™s proprietary liquefaction technology. Designed for decentralised and small-scale applications, Nordsolâ€™s system seamlessly integrates with biogas upgrading plants, transforming raw biogas into a clean, transportable, and storable fuel.
author: Analysts Account
